channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Karen Read murder case ends in a mistrial. Prosecutors say they will try again
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the case of Karen Read, a woman accused of killing her Boston police officer boyfriend by striking him with her SUV and leaving him in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the fatal police shooting of a 13-year-old boy in upstate New York
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Police in upstate New York tackled and then shot a 13-year-old boy to death after he pointed what turned out to be a BB gun at officers during a foot chase.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: José Raúl Mulino sworn in as Panama's new president, promises to stop migration through Darien Gap
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — José Raúl Mulino was sworn in Monday as Panama’s next president, facing pressure to slow irregular migration through the Darien Gap that connects his country with Colombia.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Appeals court allows part of Biden student loan repayment plan to go forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court has allowed the U.S. Education Department to move ahead with a plan to lower monthly payments for millions of student loan borrowers, putting on hold a ruling last week by a lower court. The ruling from the 10th U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the hot water that fueled Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl’s explosive growth into an unprecedented early storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season they can expect.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Things to know about the Karen Read murder case, which has ended in a mistrial
item.description: DEDHAM, Mass. (AP) — A judge declared a mistrial Monday after jurors deadlocked in the case of Karen Read, a woman accused of killing her Boston police officer boyfriend by striking him with her SUV and leaving him in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 20 riders rescued from Disney's Incredicoaster
item.description: Twenty people had to be escorted off the Disney California Adventure Park’s Incredicoaster after the ride stopped on the tracks Sunday. The stoppage occurred around 1:30 p.m. at the park located in Anaheim.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump lead over Biden unchanged since debate: Poll
item.description: Former President Trump’s lead over President Biden in national polling is unchanged following the debate between the candidates last week, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stingray that got pregnant despite no male companion has died, aquarium says
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A stingray that got pregnant at a North Carolina aquarium this winter despite not having shared a tank with a male of her species for many years has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulators
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BET says 'audio malfunction' caused heavy censorship of Usher's speech at the 2024 BET Awards
item.description: Usher's acceptance speech of the BET Awards' lifetime achievement award was heartfelt, occasionally profane and lengthy — and few people got to hear it. The 13-minute speech was largely censored by the network, leaving viewers at home curious to know what Usher said.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former NFL QB, JaMarcus Russell, sued over $74k donation intended for alma mater
item.description: Former NFL No. 1 overall draft pick and LSU standout quarterback JaMarcus Russell is facing a lawsuit over a $74,000 check written as a donation for the football program at his alma mater, Williamson High School.
item.dc:creator: Simone Eli, Fox 5

item.title: Patelco Credit Union 'serious security incident' leaves customers unable to bank online
item.description: Patelco Credit Union customers in Northern California were still waiting for answers Monday after the bank announced over the weekend that its systems experienced a serious security incident.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump golf courses' liquor licenses not renewed after conviction
item.description: The licenses expired Sunday, but the two golf clubs were issued interim permits to serve alcohol until a hearing is held on the matter. The hearing is scheduled for July 19, which the division noted is after Trump’s sentencing on July 11.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, KRON4

item.title: Fireworks believed to have caused Michigan duplex fire
item.description: The department of public safety said that one person who was inside the duplex was hurt in the fire. They were treated at the scene.
item.dc:creator: Corinne Moore, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest | Israel appears set to reinvade a city in Gaza filled with displaced civilians
item.description: The Israeli army has ordered a mass evacuation of Palestinians from the eastern half of Khan Younis. Monday’s order is a sign that Israeli troops could soon reinvade the southern Gaza city, which is currently filled with displaced civilians.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the Supreme Court immunity ruling in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court's ruling Monday in former President Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case makes it all but certain that the Republican will not face trial in Washington ahead of the November election.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WATCH: Sneaky alligator caught hiding underneath Publix shopping carts
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- Yellow caution tape surrounded the front of a Publix store last week after a sneaky little alligator was caught hiding out underneath a corral of shopping carts.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michigan woman battles 'extraordinarily rare' kidney cancer
item.description: Aiyana Mitchell, a mother of four, has Stage 4 renal medullary carcinoma, a kidney cancer that can't be cured.
item.dc:creator: Byron Tollefson, NewsNation

item.title: John Deere laying off hundreds of Midwest workers
item.description: John Deere said layoffs are being made due to reduced demand for products from factories in Illinois and Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KRON4

item.title: Family dies in New York plane crash following Cooperstown baseball tournament: police
item.description: A family of five heading back to Georgia from a baseball tournament in Cooperstown, New York, has died in a small plane crash, State Police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ThunderShirts, dance parties and anxiety meds can help ease dogs' July Fourth dread
item.description: Most of the U.S. may be looking forward to July Fourth for dazzling displays of fireworks or setting off firecrackers and poppers with their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon surrenders to federal prison to serve 4-month sentence on contempt charges
item.description: DANBURY, Conn. (AP) — Longtime Trump ally Steve Bannon was taken into custody Monday after surrendering at a federal prison to begin a four-month sentence on contempt charges for defying a subpoena in the congressional investigation into the U.S. Capitol attack.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Foxx reveals details about 2023 medical scare
item.description: Jamie Foxx has shared some new details about the 2023 medical emergency that left him unable to walk and kept him out of the public eye for several months.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Model and 'Guiding Light' actor Renauld White dies at 80
item.description: Renauld White, who began his modeling career in the 1960s, went on to appear on runways for Donna Karan, Ralph Lauren and Bill Blass.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Groom shot in the head during wedding party robbery attempt
item.description: A wedding celebration quickly turned to tragedy after a group of masked gunmen crashed the reception and shot the groom..
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, KRON4

item.title: White Nebraska man shoots and wounds 7 Guatemalan immigrant neighbors
item.description: CRETE, Neb. (AP) — A white Nebraska man described as reclusive and confrontational shot and wounded seven neighbors who are Guatemalan immigrants, and investigators said they are not ruling out racist motives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana teen murdered college administrator mom after she served him eviction notice: court docs
item.description: Connor Kobold pleaded not guilty to murder and aggravated battery charges in the death of his mother, Shanelle Burns. Kobold allegedly strangled Burns after she served him an eviction notice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Georgia firefighters found dead in baffling circumstances: police
item.description: In a Georgia mother's worst nightmare, the bodies of her missing son and his ex-girlfriend were found Sunday in Tennessee after a desperate weekslong search.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court allows key part of Biden student loan relief plan to resume
item.description: A federal appeals court on Sunday ruled that a key part of President Joe Biden’s student loan forgiveness plan can resume after an injunction was placed on the initiative.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas, KRON4

item.title: Ohio college student dies in Rome while studying abroad
item.description: Ohio college student Connor DiNapoli, a marketing major at the University of Dayton, died unexpectedly last week in Rome while studying abroad for the summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All Republican women in South Carolina Senate voted out of office
item.description: The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hunter Biden sues Fox News over miniseries
item.description: Hunter Biden has sued Fox News over a miniseries it published and then took down from its subscriber streaming service Fox Nation.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court issues ruling on Trump immunity case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects the former president could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Michael J. Fox rocks out with Coldplay at Glastonbury
item.description: Fans at Glastonbury Festival were surprised by a performance by Marty McFly. On Saturday, Michael J. Fox joined Coldplay onstage during their set at the United Kingdom festival. The band's frontman Chris Martin welcomed the actor to the front of the stage in front of 100,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Which burger chain has the most expensive prices?
item.description: Unfortunately for the casual fast food fan, prices have risen by a tremendous amount over the last two years.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WGN 9

item.title: Exotic mammal native to Central and South America spotted at Washington rest stop
item.description: Also referred to as “honey bears," the species is usually found in tropical rainforests from southern Mexico to Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, WGN 9
